assertions.expectation: Recommends combining release channels with maintenance exclusions as the approach for maximum control
assertions.reason: The response clearly recommends using Regular release channel combined with maintenance exclusions, providing both Terraform configuration and gcloud commands for maintenance exclusions

assertions.expectation: Recommends 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion to prevent disruptive changes while allowing control plane security patches. May also mention persistent exclusions with --add-maintenance-exclusion-until-end-of-support (no longer a 6-month max, auto-tracks EoS)
assertions.reason: The response only shows NO_UPGRADES exclusion scope, which blocks all upgrades including security patches. It doesn't mention the more granular 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion or the --add-maintenance-exclusion-until-end-of-support option

assertions.expectation: Recommends maintenance windows to control WHEN patches are applied (e.g., weekend windows only)
assertions.reason: The response provides detailed maintenance window configuration with weekly Sunday windows (2 AM - 6 AM UTC) in both YAML and Terraform examples

assertions.expectation: Mentions the cluster disruption budget (disruption interval) to control upgrade frequency — patch interval default 7d (max 90d), minor interval default 30d (max 90d)
assertions.reason: The response does not mention cluster disruption budget, disruption intervals, or patch/minor interval configurations at all

assertions.expectation: Suggests Stable or Extended channel for production clusters in regulated environments. Extended channel provides maximum flexibility around EoS enforcement (additional cost only during extended period)
assertions.reason: The response recommends Regular channel instead of Stable or Extended channel, and doesn't mention Extended channel's benefits for regulated environments or EoS enforcement flexibility

assertions.expectation: Notes that disabling control plane patches entirely is not recommended due to security risks — monthly patches at minimum
assertions.reason: While the response mentions security importance, it doesn't specifically warn against disabling control plane patches entirely or mention monthly patches as a minimum requirement

assertions.expectation: May mention rollout sequencing as an advanced option for controlling upgrade order across multiple clusters, and/or the cluster disruption budget for controlling CP patch frequency
assertions.reason: The response does not mention rollout sequencing or cluster disruption budget for controlling control plane patch frequency

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for configuring the recommended exclusion type and maintenance window
assertions.reason: The response provides gcloud commands for setting maintenance exclusions and temporarily modifying maintenance windows, though not for all recommended exclusion types
